items.title: Modi Pushes to Cut Import Reliance to Shield India From Shocks
items.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi is taking steps to reduce key imports into the economy in order to protect supply chains and ease pressure on the currency as geopolitical risks escalate.

items.title: Japan PE Fund Seeks $1.9 Billion Economic-Security Investments
items.description: Advantage Partners LLC plans to invest in big domestic companies posing economic security risks for Japan that might benefit from government backing and boost profits in the long term.

items.title: Korea to Announce Measures on Single-Stock Leveraged ETFs Soon
items.description: South Korea will soon announce measures to address the controversy over leveraged exchange-traded funds tied to Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc., the nation’s top financial regulator said.

items.title: IEA Boss Warns Global Economy in Peril If Hormuz Crisis Persists
items.description: The global economy faces a renewed challenge if the conflict that’s choked the Strait of Hormuz isn’t resolved in a matter of weeks, said International Energy Agency Executive Director Fatih Birol.

items.title: BHP Iron Ore Workers Stage First Port Hedland Strike Since 2000
items.description: Workers at BHP Group’s Port Hedland operations in Western Australia will stage the first strike at the company’s Pilbara iron ore hub since 2000, after last-ditch talks failed to avert industrial acti

items.title: Liquidity in Malaysia May Tighten Further as Rate-Hike Bets Grow
items.description: Funding conditions in Malaysia’s money market will likely tighten further later this year, with some analysts expecting stronger economic growth to prompt the country’s central bank to raise interest

items.title: Global Investors Hunt For Creative Backdoors Into China’s Mega CXMT IPO
items.description: Global investors largely shut out of China’s biggest IPO in nearly four years are looking at creative proxy trades as a way to profit from the country’s fast-rising memory-chip leader.

items.title: Iran War Drives New Investment in US LNG, S&P Global Says
items.description: The Iran war, and its disruptions across global energy markets, is spurring investments for new US liquefied natural gas export infrastructure, according to consulting firm S&P Global.

items.title: Li Ka-shing’s Cash-Out Plan Sets Up Empire for Leadership Shift
items.description: Billionaire Li Ka-shing and his family are preparing to usher in a new generation of leaders after some of their longest-serving lieutenants wrap up the sale of various assets to overhaul Hong Kong’s

items.title: Gold Steadies as Soft Inflation, War Risk Cloud Fed Rate Outlook
items.description: Gold steadied, as traders weighed softer-than-expected inflation data against escalating attacks in the Middle East for clues to the Federal Reserve’s interest-rate path.

items.title: BHP’s Iron Ore, Copper Output Slip as Miner Plans for Growth
items.description: BHP Group Ltd.’s iron ore production fell in the three months through June as the miner pushes ahead with expansions in copper and potash.

items.title: Asian Equities Drop Led by Kospi, Oil Extends Gain: Markets Wrap
items.description: Asian stocks declined as fresh selling in semiconductor stocks renewed concerns over the sustainability of the artificial intelligence trade. Crude oil climbed.

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 16
items.description: Oil climbed for a fourth day as the US continued attacks on Iran in a bid to secure shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: JPMorgan Leads $3 Billion Financing for Warburg’s Pantherx Buy

items.title: The Optimistic Case for the Hochul Data Center Pause
items.description: All politicians are being forced to navigate this issue.

items.title: Regional Banks, Wealth Lead Financial Services M&A
items.description: Smaller deals involving regional banks and wealth managers are driving acquisitions in the financial services industry, according to a clutch of industry watchers.

items.title: Kalshi Seeks to Let Traders Bet on Flight Cancellation Rates
items.description: Your flight? Canceled. The silver lining? You might be able to make some money off the misery.

items.title: Education Firm Anima Sinks Over 30% as Analysts Pan M&A Deal
items.description: Shares of Brazilian education company Anima Holding SA slumped a record 33% on Wednesday after it agreed to repurchase a business it had sold five years ago, with analysts citing high valuation and an

items.title: Emerging Currencies Gain as Traders Cut Back Fed Bets on US PPI
items.description: An index tracking emerging-market currencies rose Wednesday as the dollar weakened after softer-than-expected US producer prices drove traders to scale back wagers on Federal Reserve interest-rate inc

items.title: Stocks Rise as Inflation Data Outweighs Chip Rout: Markets Wrap
items.description: Another report showing softer-than-anticipated inflation drove stocks and bonds higher as Wall Street traders further dialed back wagers on Federal Reserve interest-rate increases.
